title: Role-based Wairoa dataflow sim — duty-cycle SENSOR / ROUTER+SCF / RECEIVER; F2 store-carry-forward survival, E2 distinct-origin, E3 sink-dedup, E4 silence-is-signal
topology: L1-multihop + cross-transport bridge: D1 SENSOR -> D2 ROUTER -> D3 ROUTER+BRIDGE -> D4 RECEIVER (D1<->D3 LoRa-masked; D3<->D4 ESP-NOW)
runner: hive; 4 tuxedo DFR1195 — D1=480e900e(50:26:98) SENSOR, D2=2cab5f69(b7:90:10) ROUTER, D3=f91c8911(b6:0a:a0) ROUTER+BRIDGE, D4=06ae082b(52:99:28) RECEIVER; role-by-hive_id; wairoa prov2 by composer; F2 lifecycle = my raw-serial espflash-monitor capture; step-a/happy-path/E2/E3 = composer /r2 (orch msg.* stream)
hardware: DFR1195 (ESP32-S3 + SX1262); D3 dual-radio bridge (SX1262 LoRa + onboard ESP-NOW)
config: fr4 feature (role-sim). LoRa: SF7 916.8 -9dBm + 4B prepend + hive-MASK (D1->[D2] D2->[D1,D3] D3->[D2]); ESP-NOW: ch1 broadcast + MAC-MASK (D3->[D4] D4->[D3]). F2 SCF = buffer-on-dest-unreachable (best_for(dest).is_none && !has_viable(dest, 0.3)) + forward-on-reappearance (has_viable(dest, 0.3) || path conf>0.3) + TTL-bound (SCF_TTL 120 route-s) + dedup-preserved. Outage mechanism = espflash-monitor-attach RESETS D3 -> fresh neighbour table = clean transient D4-outage (no power-cycle/stale-entry).
firmware_commit: r2-hive 884b866 + 2 metal-found fixes (SCF trigger dest-unreachable; reappearance conf>0.3 anti-churn) — staged ELFs fr4-sensor/router-bridge/receiver

expectations.predicted: a reading BUFFERED while the dest is unreachable SURVIVES and is delivered when the dest returns (the SAME buffered msg_id, not just new flow)
expectations.observed: SINGLE-ID LIFECYCLE-LINK on metal, msg_id=115 origin=D1(480e900e): D3 'SCF-HOLD msg_id=115 dest=06ae082b' (buffered for absent D4) -> D3 'SCF-FWD msg_id=115 dest=06ae082b (reappeared)' -> D4 'DELIVERED msg_id=115' dup=0 (msg.delivered{0:115,1:06ae082b,2:0,3:480e900e}). The buffered reading itself survived the outage + delivered-on-contact = TN-L2-IT-BL-000.

expectations.predicted: while the dest is absent the buffer holds STABLY (no premature forward/drop)
expectations.observed: step-a /r2 over 25s: msg.hold present (D2+D3 park), msg.scffwd=0, msg.delivered=0 — buffered-for-unreachable, nothing forwarded/dropped while D4 absent. (Required the conf>0.3 reappearance fix; the prior has_viable(.,0.0) churned the buffer empty before D4-return = a caught false-green.)

expectations.predicted: the RECEIVER delivers each reading exactly once (dedup transport-agnostic on (msg_id,origin))
expectations.observed: D4 msg.delivered dup=0 per distinct msg_id (115,116,118,119,120...); D3 dup=1 on its own flood-back echoes (dedup held). exactly_once@D4.

expectations.predicted: readings keyed by (msg_id,origin) — distinct origins don't collide
expectations.observed: all deliveries carry origin=480e900e (D1) in key 3 (fr_origin frame-carried); the dedup/correlation keys on (msg_id,origin), so a 2nd sensor would be distinct. (Single-sensor bench; E2 rides the verified (msg_id,origin) key.)

expectations.predicted: SENSOR->ROUTER->BRIDGE->RECEIVER dataflow delivers once D4 present
expectations.observed: D1->D2->D3->D4 happy path: msg.tx@D1, msg.rx/relay@D2 (directed_via D3), bridge@D3, delivered@D4 dup=0 (6 deliveries/25s). The Wairoa role-sim dataflow animates.

expectations.predicted: the RECEIVER infers ABSENCE of an expected SENSOR gone silent (msg.silence{at,origin}), no spurious delivery, clears on return
expectations.observed: D4 inferred D1's silence during D1's >30s reboot-silence (reattach-5 window): msg.silence{1:06ae082b(D4)=at, 3:480e900e(D1)=origin} — the CORRECT post-fix shape (RECEIVER=at, silent-SENSOR=origin), confirming the RECEIVER-gating fix held; then D1 resumed -> cleared. The infer-then-clear silence-is-signal lifecycle, no spurious delivery. (Observed via a real >30s D1-silence during reattach, not a deliberate stop-D1; the absence-inference mechanism is identical. A deliberate stop needs a fiddly erase-reflash for a sustained 30s+ silence — not worth the risk since the signal fired correctly with the right shape + full lifecycle.)

finding: TN-FR-4 F2 STORE-CARRY-FORWARD SURVIVAL = PROVEN on metal with the rigorous SINGLE-ID lifecycle-link (msg_id=115 held-at-D3 while D4 absent -> forwarded-on-D4-return -> delivered@D4 dup=0). This is the FIRST metal validation of the SCF-1 canon primitive (specs' SCF-STORE-CARRY-FORWARD.md), CLOSING the standing R2-ROUTE SCF gap open in the TN-L2 band. Two genuine firmware bugs were caught BY the metal runs (not in review) and fixed: (1) the SCF buffer-trigger was Drop(NoViableNeighbour) [= ZERO neighbours], but a ROUTER with other neighbours FLOODS instead -> the absent dest never triggered the buffer; fixed to dest-specific unreachability. (2) the forward-on-reappearance used has_viable(dest, 0.0) -> a decayed/stale near-zero entry passed -> the buffer CHURNED empty (forward-to-nowhere + remove) BEFORE the dest returned -> F2 would silently false-green (nothing left to deliver); fixed to a real conf>0.3 threshold. Both bugs each independently broke the F2 falsifier; the clean-test discipline (rigorous single-id capture, not suggestive endpoints) surfaced them. The reset-induced fresh-table outage mechanism (no power-cycle, no stale-entry) gave a clean reproducible D4-outage. General SCF is correct: every router that can't reach the dest buffers (D2 redundantly, TTL-drops; D3-bridge effectively forwards) — dedup-safe. The reply-direction SCF also engaged (D3 buffering D4->D1 replies), expected.
notes: F2 lifecycle = my raw-serial capture (the SCF-HOLD/FWD/DELIVERED println carry the msg_id link directly, sidestepping the orch WS-gap that ate the one-shot scffwd in the first /r2 attempt). Step-a stable-hold + happy-path + E2/E3 = composer's /r2 msg.* stream. E4 silence-is-signal is the remaining decidable signal (firmware built + RECEIVER-gated; drive = stop D1, watch D4 msg.silence) — will addend this record. Routed spec-first: the SCF impl is built against specs' SCF-1 proposal; Roy ratifies the normative section. Baseline: composer reattach-5 restores the full live demo. See [[lora-message-passing-metal]].
